description:
You are a data-analytics engineer who has been asked to debug and improve an existing CSV-processing pipeline that is meant to run in parallel on a small workstation.  
  A colleague already created a skeleton repository, but the result currently produced by the pipeline is wrong (duplicate header rows and unsorted records).  
  Your objective is to:
  
  1. Inspect the directory /home/user/project and the data directory /home/user/data/raw.  
  2. Correct /home/user/project/process_data.sh so that:  
     a. It removes the header row from every raw CSV except the first one.  
     b. It concatenates the three source files in the order sales_q1.csv, sales_q2.csv, sales_q3.csv.  
     c. It uses GNU parallel to perform all header-stripping operations in parallel (each file must be processed by a separate parallel job).  
     d. It writes the final combined file to /home/user/data/processed/all_sales_2023.csv.  
     e. It writes a deterministic processing log to /home/user/data/processed/process_log.txt in the exact five-line format shown below. Do not add or remove lines, spaces or trailing newlines; every line must end with a UNIX newline (LF).  
        Line 1: Processing started: 2023-01-01 00:00:00  
        Line 2: Input files processed: sales_q1.csv sales_q2.csv sales_q3.csv  
        Line 3: Lines written to output: 10  
        Line 4: Processing finished: 2023-01-01 00:00:00  
        Line 5: SUCCESS  
  3. Run the corrected script once so that both the output CSV and the log file are generated.  
  4. Verify that /home/user/data/processed/all_sales_2023.csv contains exactly ten lines (one header + nine data rows) in the following order and with no extra blank lines:  
     id,region,amount  
     1,North,1000  
     2,South,1500  
     3,East,800  
     4,North,1100  
     5,South,1600  
     6,West,900  
     7,East,1200  
     8,West,950  
     9,North,1300  

Important implementation constraints:  
• You must not rely on root/sudo.  
• The header-stripping must be executed with GNU parallel, not with a simple for-loop.  
• The script must remain executable (chmod +x).  
• Keep the timestamps in the log file exactly as specified (they are hard-coded on purpose so the automatic grader can perform a byte-for-byte comparison).  
• After your work, the directory /home/user/data/processed will therefore contain exactly two files: all_sales_2023.csv and process_log.txt, both matching the content described above.

Deliverables that the automated tests will look for:  
1. /home/user/data/processed/all_sales_2023.csv exists and its content is exactly the nine-row dataset shown above, preceded by one header line.  
2. /home/user/data/processed/process_log.txt exists and its content is byte-for-byte identical to the five-line template above.  
3. /home/user/project/process_data.sh is executable and its text contains the word “parallel” (case-sensitive), proving that you implemented the parallel execution requirement.

Work entirely in the terminal; do not edit any files outside the given directories.  
When everything checks out, you are done.
truth:
Initial state before the agent starts:

  1. Directory layout
     /home/user/
     ├── data/
     │   ├── raw/
     │   │   ├── sales_q1.csv
     │   │   ├── sales_q2.csv
     │   │   └── sales_q3.csv
     │   └── processed/        (empty, already exists, 0755)
     └── project/
         └── process_data.sh   (0755)

  2. Contents of the three raw CSV files:

     /home/user/data/raw/sales_q1.csv
     id,region,amount
     1,North,1000
     2,South,1500
     3,East,800

     /home/user/data/raw/sales_q2.csv
     id,region,amount
     4,North,1100
     5,South,1600
     6,West,900

     /home/user/data/raw/sales_q3.csv
     id,region,amount
     7,East,1200
     8,West,950
     9,North,1300

  3. Faulty original version of /home/user/project/process_data.sh

     #!/bin/bash
     # WRONG: simply concatenates files and duplicates headers
     cat /home/user/data/raw/*.csv > /home/user/data/processed/all_sales_2023.csv
     echo "done"

  Required final state after the agent finishes:

  A. /home/user/data/processed/all_sales_2023.csv with exactly the following 10 lines (including newline at the end of the last line):

     id,region,amount
     1,North,1000
     2,South,1500
     3,East,800
     4,North,1100
     5,South,1600
     6,West,900
     7,East,1200
     8,West,950
     9,North,1300

  B. /home/user/data/processed/process_log.txt with exactly these 5 lines (each ending with \n):

     Processing started: 2023-01-01 00:00:00
     Input files processed: sales_q1.csv sales_q2.csv sales_q3.csv
     Lines written to output: 10
     Processing finished: 2023-01-01 00:00:00
     SUCCESS

  C. /home/user/project/process_data.sh is executable (mode 0755) and its contents include the string “parallel”.

  Automated tests will:
     • md5sum the CSV and the log file against the literal text above.
     • grep -q "parallel" /home/user/project/process_data.sh
